items.standfirst: Prosecutors drop two other charges against Jasper Jones author, 43, including allegations he produced child exploitation materialAward‑winning author Craig Silvey has pleaded guilty to possessing and distributing child exploitation material, while a charge that he produced such material has been dropped.Silvey, 43, was first charged in January after detectives from Western Australia police’s child abuse squad raided his Fremantle home, allegedly catching him communicating online with child exploitation offenders and seizing his electronic devices. Continue reading...
items.content: Prosecutors drop two other charges against Jasper Jones author, 43, including allegations he produced child exploitation materialAward‑winning author Craig Silvey has pleaded guilty to possessing and distributing child exploitation material, while a charge that he produced such material has been dropped.Silvey, 43, was first charged in January after detectives from Western Australia police’s child abuse squad raided his Fremantle home, allegedly catching him communicating online with child exploitation offenders and seizing his electronic devices. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Follow our live coverage of news in Australia and around the globe.
items.content: Follow our live coverage of news in Australia and around the globe.

items.title: Author Craig Silvey admits child exploitation crimes
items.standfirst: Australian author Craig Silvey has pleaded guilty to possessing and distributing child exploitation material, as prosecutors dropped two other charges.
items.content: Award‑winning author Craig Silvey has pleaded guilty to possessing and distributing child exploitation material, while a charge that he produced such material has been dropped.Silvey, 43, was first charged in January after detectives from Western Australian Police's Child Abuse Squad raided his Fremantle home, allegedly catching him communicating online with child exploitation offenders and seizing his electronic devices.The father of three was later hit with extra charges, including allegations he produced child exploitation material between February and June 2022, and possessed further material on January 12 this year.LIVE UPDATES: Australians to feel the pain of oil price surge as ceasefire breaksThose two additional charges have now been discontinued, and Silvey has admitted the remaining counts of possessing and distributing child exploitation material linked to offences in January.Silvey faced the Fremantle Magistrates Court on Tuesday when he entered guilty pleas.The popular author is best known for his 2009 coming‑of‑age novel Jasper Jones, which is considered a modern Australian classic and has been adapted into a feature film and several stage productions.He has also been widely recognised for other books, including Rhubarb, Honeybee, which won the Australian Indie Book Award in 2021, and the children's novel Runt, which was also made into a film.Publishers Allen & Unwin and Fremantle Press, which released Rhubarb, stopped promoting his books after the original charges were laid and most of his titles were removed from reading lists across the country.Silvey's bail was continued, and he is next due to appear in the District Court on July 3 for sentencing.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Renowned Australian author pleads guilty to two child exploitation offences
items.standfirst: Author Craig Silvey, best known for his books Jasper Jones and Runt, pleads guilty to distributing and possessing child exploitation material.
items.content: Author Craig Silvey, best known for his books Jasper Jones and Runt, pleads guilty to distributing and possessing child exploitation material.

items.standfirst: The driver of a stolen ute hid from police for more than two hours after the crash, which claimed the life of a 60-year-old.
items.content: The driver of a stolen ute hid from police for more than two hours after the crash, which claimed the life of a 60-year-old.

items.title: Woman dead, man in custody after stolen car crashes north of Brisbane
items.standfirst: T he driver of the stolen ute fled on foot to a nearby school, and is now in custody.
items.content: A woman has died and a man is in custody after a stolen car caused a head-on crash north of Brisbane this morning.The collision happened at Archer Street near Victoria Lane in Woodford, Caboolture, just after 6am.Emergency services were told a white Isuzu D-max ute and an orange Toyota Corolla had collided head-on.LIVE UPDATES: US, Israel planning 'massive' strikes on Iran The ute was reported stolen from a home on McLoughlin Road in Morayfield at around 3.45am.The driver of the Toyota, a 60-year-old woman, suffered critical injuries and died at the scene.The driver of the stolen ute, a 31-year-old man, fled on foot, but was found by police at a nearby state school which was placed into lockdown."The department can confirm Woodford State School underwent a brief lockdown today due to a suspected intruder on school grounds," a Queensland Education spokesperson said."Queensland Police Service attended to search school grounds. The lockdown was lifted at 9.10am on police advice and classes resumed as normal."The man was taken into custody and is co-operating with police.Investigations are ongoing.
